steps.label: Loading TCR profile
steps.detail: CDR3β: CASSIVLAGSNEQFF | TRBV19 | TRBJ2-1 | Source: Gate et al. 2020 plate-seq (AD CSF)

steps.label: Checking clonal expansion
steps.detail: 31 cells from patient AD1 — 34% of ALL CSF T cells. This is extraordinary clonal expansion.

steps.label: Searching ESM-2 nearest neighbors
steps.detail: Computing cosine similarity across 89,221 TCR embeddings (1,280 dimensions). 4/10 annotated neighbors are viral, but the base rate for viral in annotated TCRs is 43% — this is expected, not evidence of viral specificity.

steps.label: Checking antigen databases
steps.detail: VDJdb: No exact CDR3β match. McPAS: No match. TRAIT: No match. This TCR is TRUE dark matter — its target is completely unknown.

steps.label: DecoderTCR binding prediction
steps.detail: ⚠️ Beta-only chain — cannot run DecoderTCR (requires paired alpha+beta). This limitation IS the story: the most expanded T cell in AD CSF, and we can't predict what it sees.

steps.label: Literature context
steps.detail: Gate 2020 Nature: clonal CD8+ T cell expansion in AD CSF. Bjornevik 2022 Science: EBV → 32× MS risk. Lanz 2022 Nature: EBNA1 mimics GlialCAM. Gate 2021 Science: T cells react to α-synuclein in Lewy body dementia.

steps.label: Generating hypothesis
steps.detail: A T cell clearly doing something important (34% of CSF!) but target completely unknown. Neighbor annotations are indistinguishable from database background. Could be: CNS autoantigen, viral peptide (EBV?), or stress neoepitope. This is exactly the kind of TCR that needs TCRAFT synthesis (<$1/TCR) and library-on-library screening to solve.

steps.label: Loading TCR profile
steps.detail: CDR3β: CASSQGLAEETQYF | Source: Gaglione et al. 2026 Immunity (vitiligo skin biopsy). Paired alpha: CALSEGAGGFKTIF.

steps.label: Searching ESM-2 nearest neighbors
steps.detail: Computing cosine similarity across 89,221 TCR embeddings (1,280 dimensions). 9/10 nearest neighbors are unannotated dark matter — this region of embedding space is largely unexplored. Cross-disease convergence: similar CDR3 motifs (CASS[x]GL[x]E[x]TQYF) appear in PDAC, AD CSF, and McPAS datasets.

steps.label: DecoderTCR panel scoring (14 epitopes)
steps.detail: Scoring against melanocyte antigens (TRP-2, MART-1, gp100, TYR), cancer (NY-ESO-1, PRAME), and viral controls (CMV, EBV, Flu, COVID, HIV)...

steps.label: Top hit: TRP-2/SVYDFFVWL
steps.detail: Score: +0.514 | Null distribution (N=10,000): p=0.0001 | Exceeds ALL 10,000 scrambled CDR3 scores! Specificity: EBV p=0.26 (ns), CMV p=0.22 (ns) — TRP-2 signal is epitope-specific.

steps.label: Consistency check: neighborhood ↔ prediction
steps.detail: Neighborhood is mostly dark matter — no annotated melanocyte neighbors to cross-validate against. However, the cross-disease CDR3 motif convergence (PDAC, AD CSF, McPAS all share this motif) suggests a conserved binding specificity. The primary evidence for TRP-2 binding is the 10K-scramble null distribution (p=0.0001) and epitope specificity controls (EBV/CMV not significant).

steps.label: CDR3β mutagenesis scan
steps.detail: Scoring all 240 single-amino-acid mutations (12 positions × 20 AAs). Best mutation: S3H (+0.018 delta). Sensitive positions: A1, S2, S3. Modest deltas suggest CDR3 is already near-optimal from in vivo selection.

steps.label: Biological interpretation
steps.detail: TRP-2 is a melanocyte differentiation antigen shared between vitiligo and melanoma. Two strong lines of evidence: (1) DecoderTCR binding score p=0.0001 (10,000 scrambled controls), (2) epitope-specific signal (TRP-2 yes, EBV/CMV no). The CDR3 motif also converges across 3 disease contexts. Ranks #1 of 201 TCRAFT TCRs (99.5th percentile).

steps.label: Synthesis recommendation
steps.detail: High-confidence candidate. TCRAFT pooled synthesis: <$1/TCR. Recommend WT + top 3 mutagenesis variants for experimental validation.

steps.label: Loading TCR profile
steps.detail: CDR3β: CASSLGQAYEQYF | Source: Gate et al. 2020 (Alzheimer's CSF) | 14 cells across 3 patients

steps.label: Searching ESM-2 nearest neighbors
steps.detail: Computing cosine similarity across 89,221 TCR embeddings. 6/10 annotated neighbors are viral — but with 43% base rate, this is expected for any TCR near VDJdb entries.

steps.label: Cross-reference against VDJdb
steps.detail: 🎯 EXACT CDR3β MATCH in VDJdb: CASSLGQAYEQYF → EBNA3A/FLRGRAYGL (EBV). This is experimentally validated! The database match is the primary evidence — the neighbor enrichment is expected for identical sequences.

steps.label: HLA restriction check
steps.detail: ⚠️ FLRGRAYGL is HLA-B*08:01 restricted, NOT HLA-A*02:01. DecoderTCR binding scores unreliable for this epitope. But the VDJdb CDR3β match is HLA-independent evidence.

steps.label: Literature context: EBV → neurodegeneration
steps.detail: Bjornevik 2022 Science: EBV → 32× MS risk. Lanz 2022 Nature: EBNA1 mimics GlialCAM. Gate 2020: EBV-reactive TCRs in AD CSF via GLIPH clustering. Our finding recapitulates Gate 2020 using sequence matching.

steps.label: Discovery significance
steps.detail: We recaptured the Gate 2020 finding via CDR3β database matching. An Alzheimer's CSF T cell has the exact same receptor as a known EBV-reactive clone. This extends the EBV→neurodegeneration paradigm and validates our cross-referencing approach.

steps.label: Loading TCR profile
steps.detail: CDR3β: CASSQGYEQYF | TRBV11-2 | TRBJ2-7 | Source: Gaglione et al. 2026 Immunity (vitiligo skin biopsy). Has paired alpha chain.

steps.label: Searching ESM-2 nearest neighbors
steps.detail: Computing cosine similarity across 89,221 TCR embeddings. Neighbors include 2/10 autoimmune-associated TCRs (5% base rate).

steps.label: Cross-reference against VDJdb
steps.detail: 🎯 EXACT CDR3β MATCH in VDJdb: CASSQGYEQYF → KLFEFLVYGV/SMARCD3 (HLA-A*02:01). This is a SELF-ANTIGEN — a chromatin remodeling protein expressed in all human cells.

steps.label: Self-antigen implications
steps.detail: SMARCD3 (SWI/SNF complex member) is ubiquitously expressed. A T cell in vitiligo skin targeting a self-protein is consistent with autoimmunity — but SMARCD3 is NOT melanocyte-specific. May represent broader tissue-agnostic autoimmunity.

steps.label: Reclassification
steps.detail: Status change: Dark matter → Validated self-antigen target. Evidence: exact CDR3β match in VDJdb (experimentally validated). The TCRAFT screen missed this because SMARCD3 wasn't in their peptide panel. The agent found it in seconds by database cross-referencing.

steps.label: Loading TCR profile
steps.detail: CDR3β: CASSSSGSSYNEQFF | Source: Gaglione et al. 2026 Immunity (pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma TILs). This T cell was inside a pancreatic tumor.

steps.label: Searching ESM-2 nearest neighbors
steps.detail: Computing cosine similarity across 89,221 TCR embeddings. 5/10 annotated neighbors are viral — suspicious for a tumor-infiltrating T cell, though consistent with 43% viral base rate.

steps.label: Cross-reference against VDJdb
steps.detail: 🎯 EXACT CDR3β MATCH in VDJdb: CASSSSGSSYNEQFF → RPIIRPATL/NP (Influenza B). This tumor-infiltrating T cell is flu-reactive! The database match is definitive.

steps.label: Bystander analysis
steps.detail: This T cell infiltrated the tumor but wasn't recruited by tumor antigens. It expanded during a prior flu infection and trafficked non-specifically. Bystanders constitute 30-80% of TILs in solid tumors (Simoni 2018 Nature, Scheper 2019 Nature Medicine).

steps.label: Clinical significance
steps.detail: Identifying bystanders is critical for TIL therapy — expanding flu-specific T cells won't fight cancer. The agent unmasked this via CDR3β database matching. Dark matter → Viral bystander. Filtering bystanders from true tumor-reactive TILs is exactly the kind of problem the TCRAFT screen solves at scale.
